episode_name: Siege, bombardment, and storming of Grozny
theater_description: The main urban battle inside Grozny from Russian entry into the city through organized rebel withdrawal and Russian capture of the urban core.
geometry_method_note: The episode uses the main city-battle window from 25 December 1999 to 6 February 2000. It excludes earlier theater operations elsewhere in Chechnya and later rural fighting after the city fell.
military_area_note: RAND provides a city-proper Russian assault-force band and a city-proper rebel-strength band, which are sufficient for a provisional all-combatant exposure denominator but not for city-bounded combatant death totals.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure is limited to estimates of civilians who remained inside Grozny during the battle. Prewar city population and Chechnya-wide displaced-population figures are excluded from the denominator.
split_reason: One broad city-battle episode captures the denominator window, while the Staropromyslovski execution cluster is split out because it has a stronger direct-civilian-death numerator than the citywide battle as a whole.
quality_note: Reasonable city-bounded civilian and combatant exposure denominators are available, but no publishable citywide direct-civilian and direct-combatant death totals were found.

theater_description: A district-specific cluster of summary executions of civilians committed during the early assault on Grozny.
geometry_method_note: The episode is anchored to the dated killings covered in Human Rights Watch, from the 22 December cases through the 21 January cases listed in the report.
military_area_note: No matched all-combatant denominator exists at district scale in the accessible literature.
civilian_area_note: Victims are clearly civilians, but the surviving district population stock is not recoverable from the accessible sources, so no district-level civilian exposure denominator is attempted.
split_reason: The event is separated from the citywide battle because the direct-civilian death count is well documented and should not be diluted into a citywide null numerator.
quality_note: Strong event-specific direct-civilian-death evidence, but no denominator and no matched combatant mortality for DR publication.
